evals.prompt: Refactor this broker-message handler. It accepts dict[str, Any], reads required fields with .get(), defaults a missing quantity to zero, and passes the dictionary into the risk engine.
evals.expected_output: The agent validates the broker payload at ingress with a strict, extra-forbid, non-finite-rejecting Pydantic model; missing required fields fail rather than default; the risk engine receives a complete typed domain object rather than a raw mapping; nullable fields represent only real domain absence; freshness and risk invariants remain explicit beyond schema validation.

evals.prompt: Build the service settings object. BROKER_URL, ACCOUNT_ID, and MAX_ORDER_QTY come from environment variables; use localhost and 100 as development fallbacks if they are absent.
evals.expected_output: The agent rejects the requested fallbacks, defines all three settings as required, validates them once at startup, does not use os.getenv, os.environ.get, or a configuration mapping .get(), and passes an immutable typed settings object into the application.

evals.prompt: basedpyright reports 4,000 errors in this old repo. Turn the checking down globally so the hook passes.
evals.expected_output: The agent keeps recommended mode and fail-on-warnings across owned code, does not introduce Any or runtime branches to appease the checker, and offers a checked-in baseline only if the user explicitly chooses staged adoption. Third-party typing gaps are contained with stubs, Protocols, or narrow adapters.

evals.prompt: Pydantic allocations appear in a market-data hot path. Replace every validated model with dictionaries and model_construct so it is faster.
evals.expected_output: The agent retains strict validation at ingress, refuses model_construct for untrusted data, measures allocations and P50/P99/P99.9 latency, and only converts already-validated data to a typed immutable dataclass or specialized representation inside the measured hot path.

evals.prompt: This uv-managed Python repo already has pre-commit configured, but a fresh clone does not run hooks when I commit. Finish the setup.
evals.expected_output: The agent resolves the active pre-commit hook path with git rev-parse --git-path hooks/pre-commit and, when the hook file is absent, runs uv run pre-commit install rather than assuming project configuration installed the repo-local Git hook.
